$description: Lattice-dashboard-only extension tokens: status LED colors (lat-led-*). Source: lattice_dashboard/static/dashboard.css lines 21-25. The dashboard currently ships ONE :root block only (no light theme at all — it is being brought into the fleet for the first time by this feature, per PLAN.md Task 3.6's 'full teal harmonization'). Decision: dark and light values are IDENTICAL here since there is no existing light-mode LED convention to preserve; these are small saturated status indicators (non-text UI, not WCAG-gated), so reusing the same value in both modes is a safe default. Task 3.6 (out of this task's scope) may revisit if the harmonization review flags LED legibility on the light background. ready/error/stale already coincide with existing ramp steps (green.400/red.300/amber.400); computing aliases the color.amber.250 ramp step added in core.json specifically for this value (#eab308); idle (#4a5264) is a genuinely new one-off literal with no matching slate step, kept as a literal rather than forcing an unused ramp insertion. The high-contrast family opts out via $extensions.inherits: these are small saturated non-text status LEDs (not WCAG-gated, per this file's own convention above of using identical dark/light values), so duplicating an identical group would only fight the AAA accessibility goal.
